steps.title: Graph the two equations
steps.content: Enter the two equations as written. The $x$-coordinates of their intersection points are the possible values of $x$ for the system.

steps.title: Check the listed value
steps.content:
Test $x=-12$ by substituting it into the linear equation to get $y$.
$$-3(-12)+y=3$$
$$36+y=3$$
$$y=-33$$

steps.title: Verify the quadratic equation
steps.content: Check the same $x$ and $y$ in the other equation. $$(-12)^2+(-33)=144-33=111$$ So $x=-12$ is a possible value. The answer is A.
